cells.cell_text: Number of medications at discharge ± SD

page.content: In recent years, the increasing prevalence of drug-related health issues has raised significant concerns about the impact of substance use on hospital readmissions. The accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of readmission rates, delineating between drug-related and non-drug-related cases. Notably, 90 cases, representing 21.0% of total readmissions, were attributed to drug-related factors, while a substantial 339 cases, or 79.0%, were classified as non-drug-related. This stark contrast highlights the urgent need for targeted interventions to address the underlying causes of drug-related readmissions. Furthermore, the table presents odds ratios (OR) for both simple and multiple models, accompanied by 95% confidence intervals (CI), which offer valuable insights into the relative risks associated with readmissions in these two categories. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for healthcare providers and policymakers as they develop strategies aimed at reducing the burden of readmissions. By focusing on factors contributing to drug-related readmissions, healthcare systems can implement more effective prevention and treatment programs, ultimately improving patient outcomes and resource allocation within the healthcare framework.
